2015-09-26.abstract: At the last minute, he said, a rebel leader tried to back out of a crucial part of the deal, a deadline to sign a final accord.

2015-09-27.title: Partnership Aims to Bolster Local Health Care Providers
2015-09-27.abstract: A collaboration of the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation, the World Bank and the World Health Organization will focus on medical care in poor and middle-income countries.

2015-09-27.title: President Xi Jinping of China Pledges $2 Billion to Fight Poverty
2015-09-27.abstract: Mr. Xi told the United Nations that China would pledge $2 billion as an initial investment for development assistance to the poorest countries.

2015-09-27.title: 1915: Russian Cavalry Entraps Enemy
2015-09-27.abstract: From the International Herald Tribune archives: Fighting of extraordinary violence continues in Russia in 1915.

2015-09-27.title: France Says Its Airstrikes Hit an ISIS Camp in Syria
2015-09-27.abstract: President François Hollande said in a statement that the airstrikes had been coordinated with other nations battling the extremist group but gave few other details.

2015-09-27.title: 1965: Jesuit’s Chief Says Atheism Invades Church
2015-09-27.abstract: From the International Herald Tribune archive: The head of the Jesuists warns against a “new godless society.”

2015-09-28.title: Chinese President Pushes Gender Equality
2015-09-28.abstract: At a United Nations conference of global leaders, President Xi Jinping of China proposed a plan to support women’s development worldwide.

2015-09-28.title: Limited Progress Seen Even as More Nations Step Up on Climate
2015-09-28.abstract: The pledges to battle climate change would still allow the world to heat up to a level that is likely to produce catastrophes, scientists say.

2015-09-28.title: Taiwan Braces for Arrival of Powerful Typhoon Dujuan
2015-09-28.abstract: The storm, which is bringing heavy rains and strong winds, is the second major typhoon to hit the region in less than two months.

2015-09-28.title: At the U.N., Raúl Castro of Cuba Calls for End to U.S. Embargo
2015-09-28.abstract: President Raúl Castro of Cuba called for an end to the economic embargo of his country on Monday at the United Nations General Assembly.

2015-09-28.title: Obama and Putin Play Diplomatic Poker Over Syria
2015-09-28.abstract: President Obama and President Vladimir V. Putin essentially blamed each other for the catastrophic war in Syria and the refugee crisis it spurred in their speeches at the General Assembly.

2015-09-29.title: The Syria Problem at the United Nations
2015-09-29.abstract: At the 70th annual session of the United Nations General Assembly, world leaders addressed the war in Syria, which has contributed to the rise to ISIS and a global migrant crisis.

2015-09-29.title: Obama and Putin Play Diplomatic Poker Over Syria
2015-09-29.abstract: Presidents Obama and Vladimir V. Putin clashed at the United Nations, each trying to maneuver the other into shifting his position on the Syrian leader, Bashar al-Assad.

2015-09-29.title: Rallying Global Support, Obama Pledges Larger U.S. Role in Peacekeeping Missions
2015-09-29.abstract: The president’s announcement followed a pledge from China that it would build an 8,000-member peacekeeping standby force and contribute $1 billion to a United Nations “peace and development fund.”

2015-09-29.title: Lebanon: TV Executive Is Fined in Case Tied to Hariri Tribunal
2015-09-29.abstract: Karma al-Khayat, an executive with Al Jadeed TV in Lebanon, was fined 10,000 euros — more than $11,000 — for contempt of court.

2015-09-29.title: Typhoon Dujuan Batters Taiwan With Heavy Rain and Winds
2015-09-29.abstract: The storm killed three people and left hundreds injured, Taiwan’s National Fire Agency said.

2015-09-30.title: Shinzo Abe Addresses U.N.
2015-09-30.abstract: The Japanese prime minister pledged to increase peacekeeping assistance in the Middle East and urged states with nuclear weapons to work toward nuclear disarmament.

2015-09-30.title: Russia Buildup Seen as Fanning Flames in Syria
2015-09-30.abstract: On the long list of Moscow’s goals in building up its military presence in Syria, taking on the Islamic State is but one entry, foreign policy experts say.

2015-09-30.title: John Kerry Rushes In Where Obama Will Not Tread
2015-09-30.abstract: The idea that solving the crisis in Syria, or any diplomatic dilemma, may be elusive, perhaps even impossible, is no deterrent to Secretary of State John Kerry.

2015-09-30.title: Japan: China Detains 2 Japanese on Suspicion of Espionage
2015-09-30.abstract: Asked if Japan had sent spies to China, a government spokesman said, “As a nation, we absolutely do not do that. I want to say the same thing to all countries.”

2015-09-30.title: More Than 14 Years After U.S. Invasion, the Taliban Control Large Parts of Afghanistan
2015-09-30.abstract: At least one-fifth of the country is controlled or contested by the Taliban.
